description_long: This is a freestanding clock tower constructed primarily from dark brown wood, featuring a tall, slender, hexagonal column that rises from a wide, hexagonal base. At the top, the tower is capped with a broad, overhanging, multi-tiered pyramidal roof, providing a distinct architectural silhouette. The clock face is circular, set into a square wooden housing just below the roof, and is clearly visible on one side of the tower. The clock face displays black Arabic numerals and black hour and minute hands against a white background, with a simple, functional design. The wood surface appears matte and slightly textured, suggesting a weather-resistant finish suitable for outdoor urban environments. There are no visible doors, windows, or decorative embellishments beyond the clock face and the geometric roof structure.
description: A tall, hexagonal wooden clock tower with a broad pyramidal roof and a single circular clock face with Arabic numerals. The design is simple and functional, with a matte brown finish and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the clock face clearly, with the hexagonal base and the overhanging pyramidal roof visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view, still showing the clock face but with more emphasis on the left side of the hexagonal column and base.
description_view_2: Rear view with the clock face still visible, but the right side of the column and base are more prominent.
description_view_3: Angled view highlighting the right side of the tower, with the clock face visible and the geometric base and roof structure apparent.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular with a white background, black Arabic numerals marking each hour, and two black hands for hours and minutes. The design is simple and functional, with no additional decorative elements or markings.
